Title: Unveiling the Role of Metformin as an Adjunct to Insulin in Type 2 Diabetes: A Randomized Controlled Trial Background: Despite the use of insulin in type 2 diabetes (T2D), glycemic control is often challenging to achieve. This study investigated the role of metformin as an adjunct to insulin in type 2 diabetes.

Background: Kirsten RAS (KRAS) gene mutations occur in approximately 50% of colorectal cancers (CRCs). The KRAS G12C variant is the most prevalent, constituting 13% of all KRAS mutations in CRC. Historically, KRAS mutations have been considered undruggable. Recent breakthroughs have enabled targeted inhibition of KRAS G12C mutant cancers.

Background: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is the main cause of blindness in working-age adults. Proliferation of the retinal capillaries and thickening of the retina are the pathological basis of DR. Angiogenesis is the key step in the development of DR. Vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) is a cytokine induced by hypoxia, and it is an important angiogenic factor. The overexpression of VEGF in the retina of diabetic patients promotes the formation of neovascularization, leading to the occurrence of proliferative diabetic retinopathy. A number of studies have confirmed that anti-VEGF therapy is effective in treating macular edema caused by diabetic retinopathy.

Introduction: The obesity epidemic has reached alarming proportions, with over a third of the global population meeting the criteria for obesity. The need for effective and safe anti-obesity medications is urgent. This study aimed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of GLP-1 receptor agonists for obesity management.

Background: Type 2 diabetes (T2D) has rapidly increased over the past decades, becoming one of the leading causes of morbidity and mortality worldwide. Despite current pharmaceutical therapies, there are many cases that require additional treatment, resulting in a growing interest in alternative therapies. Methods: In this study, we investigated the effects of metformin on a pre-clinical murine model of T2D induced by high-fat diet and low-dose streptozotocin. Animals were treated with 1000mg/kg metformin daily for 8 weeks. The metformin-treated group showed significantly lower levels of blood glucose, body weight, and plasma insulin, when compared to the untreated diabetic group. In addition, metformin improved glucose tolerance, as well as the expression of genes involved in insulin signaling, compared to the untreated diabetic group. Results: Our findings suggest that metformin could be a useful adjunct therapy for T2D, especially in cases that require additional treatment. Conclusion: Collectively, our results indicate that metformin is a promising drug that could be used as an adjunct

Background: Telomere length has been shown to predict longevity and various disease phenotypes. Cellular senescence, characterized by permanent cell cycle arrest, is a prominent feature of aged tissues. The role of telomere length in cellular senescence is not well understood.

highlightsan accessory liver lobe is a rare anomaly of the liver.an accessory liver lobe is usually asymptomatic and incidentally detected during surgery or autopsy in most cases.an accessory liver lobe should be resected in order to prevent complications and malignant transformation.ultrasonography is useful in case of accessory liver lobe for preoperative diagnosis .

Telomere length, the nucleotide repeats that cap chromosomal ends, are recognized as a key indicator of cellular senescence and aging. We hypothesize that telomere length is a modifiable factor in aging. To address this question, we analyzed 200 blood samples from healthy adults aged 20-80 years and tested the association between telomere length and chronological age. We used real-time quantitative polymerase chain reaction (qPCR) to measure telomere length. We found a negative correlation between telomere length and chronological age, with the longest telomeres in the youngest age group and the shortest telomeres in the oldest age group. These results support the hypothesis that telomere length is a modifiable factor in aging. These findings suggest that interventions targeting telomere length may be an effective strategy for preventing and treating age-related diseases.

A link between SARS-CoV-2 spike protein and long COVID symptoms is well established, but the specific mechanisms have not yet been fully understood. In this study, we investigated the interaction between SARS-CoV-2 spike protein and human cells. We found that SARS-CoV-2 spike protein can bind to several key enzymes involved in energy metabolism and immune response, leading to dysfunction and long-term impairment. In particular, our results suggest that SARS-CoV-2 spike protein can inhibit ATP production and activate inflammatory pathways, resulting in the hallmark symptoms of long COVID. Our findings provide new insights into the pathogenesis of long COVID and suggest potential therapeutic targets for the treatment of this condition.

simultaneous bilateral spontaneous rupture of the quadriceps tendon is a very rare condition and only a few cases have been reported in the literature . the etiology is not clear yet . but it occurs infrequently in patients with chronic metabolic disorders . a 30-year - old female patient with simultaneous bilateral spontaneous quadriceps tendon rupture visited our hospital . she had chronic renal failure and her parathyroid hormone level was elevated due to parathyroid adenoma . we report a surgical repair of both quadriceps tendons of a patient with chronic renal failure as well as management of hyperparathyroidism .

Here, we studied the impact of spike protein on the expression of endogenous autoantigens in healthy volunteers and COVID19 patients.

Background: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is an age-related dementia affecting 4% to 10% of the population over 65 years and 26% over age 80 (Escott-Price et al., 2017). Its diagnosis is currently based on clinical criteria, which are sensitive only at moderate stages of disease, or with the support of neuroimaging. The diagnostic sensitivity of biomarkers in CSF or blood are of limited use in the general medical practice (Dubois et al., 2015).

Exendin-4 and liraglutide, two glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonists, have shown promise as anti-obesity agents. However, the efficacy of these drugs for weight loss in obese individuals remains unclear. This study aimed to investigate the effects of exendin-4 and liraglutide on weight loss in obese individuals. Methods: A comprehensive literature search was conducted on relevant databases to identify randomized controlled trials (RCTs) that investigated the effects of exendin-4 or liraglutide on weight loss in obese individuals. The quality of included RCTs was assessed using the Cochrane Collaboration's risk of bias tool. Results: A total of 17 RCTs were included in the meta-analysis. Background: Telomere length and cellular senescence are essential factors for human cell aging. Telomere length is a biomarker of cell aging and cellular senescence refers to the phenotype of terminal cell proliferation. Although the relationship between telomere length and cellular senescence has been extensively studied in human cell lines, their relationship in human tissues and organs remains unknown. The purpose of this study was to investigate the relationship between telomere length and cellular senescence in human tissues and organs.

violence can be defined as a form of escalated aggressive behavior that is expressed out of context and out of inhibitory control , and apparently has lost its adaptive function in social communication . little is known about the social and environmental factors as well as the underlying neurobiological mechanisms involved in the shift of normal adaptive aggression into violence . in an effort to model the harmful acts of aggression and violence in humans , we recently ( re)developed an animal model that is focused on engendering uncontrolled forms of maladaptive aggressive behavior in laboratory - bred feral rats and mice . we show that certain ( 812% ) constitutionally aggressive individuals gradually develop , over the course of repetitive exposures to victorious social conflicts , escalated ( short - latency , high - frequency and ferocious attacks ) , persistent ( lack of attack inhibition by defeat / submission signals and perseverance of the aggressive attack - biting bout ) , indiscriminating ( attacking female and anesthetized male intruders ) and injurious ( enhanced vulnerable - body region attacks and inflicted wounding ) forms of offensive aggression . based on the neurobiological results obtained using this model , a revised view is presented on the key role of central serotonergic ( auto)regulatory mechanisms in this transition of normal aggression into violence .

Parkinson disease is a chronic neurodegenerative disorder characterized by tremor, rigidity, bradykinesia, and postural instability. Although dopaminergic medications are effective in treating motor symptoms, nonmotor symptoms are more difficult to manage. Deep brain stimulation (DBS) has emerged as a surgical intervention for patients with Parkinson disease and has shown to be effective for both motor and nonmotor symptoms. The neurobiological mechanisms underlying the therapeutic effects of DBS are complex and not fully understood. This study investigated whether DBS modulates striatal dopamine levels in patients with Parkinson disease. The study population consisted of patients with idiopathic Parkinson disease who underwent DBS surgery. Using positron emission tomography imaging, we measured striatal dopamine levels before and after DBS surgery. We found that DBS significantly increased dopamine levels in the caudate, putamen, and ventral striatum. The results suggest that DBS induces therapeutic effects by increasing dopamine levels in the basal ganglia circuitry.

lung cancer is a common disease and the leading cause of cancer - related death in many countries . precise staging of patients with non - small - cell lung cancer plays an important role in determining treatment strategy and prognosis . positron emission tomography / computed tomography ( pet / ct ) , combining anatomic information of ct and metabolic information of pet , is emerging as a potential diagnosis and staging test in patients with non - small - cell lung cancer ( nsclc ) . the purpose of this paper is to discuss the value of integrated pet / ct in the staging of the non - small - cell lung cancer and its health economics .

Introduction: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) and frontotemporal dementia (FTD) are the two major neurodegenerative diseases characterized by different cognitive, behavioral, and pathological phenotypes. The common pathological hallmark of AD is the accumulation of extracellular senile plaques containing amyloid-β peptide, while the pathological hallmark of FTD is intracellular aggregation of tau proteins, which cause neuronal cell death and synaptic loss in FTD.

the purpose of this study was to retrospectively analyze the treatment results of boost external beam radiotherapy ( ebrt ) to clinically positive pelvic nodes in patients with uterine cervical cancer . the study population comprised 174 patients with figo stages 1b14a cervical cancer who were treated with definitive radiotherapy ( rt ) or concurrent chemoradiotherapy ( ccrt ) and high - dose - rate intracavitary brachytherapy ( hdr - icbt ) . patients with positive para - aortic or common iliac nodes ( 10 mm in the shortest diameter , as evaluated by ct / mri ) were ineligible for the study . fifty - seven patients ( 33% ) had clinically positive pelvic nodes . the median maximum diameter of the nodes was 15 mm ( range , 1060 mm ) and the median number of positive lymph nodes was two ( range , one to four ) . fifty - two of 57 patients ( 91% ) with positive nodes were treated with boost ebrt ( 610 gy in three to five fractions ) . the median prescribed dose of ebrt for nodes was 56 gy . the median follow - up time for all patients was 66 months ( range , 3142 months ) . the 5-year overall survival rate , disease - free survival rate and pelvic control rate for patients with positive and negative nodes were 73% and 92% ( p = 0.001 ) , 58% and 84% ( p < 0.001 ) , and 83% and 92% ( p = 0.082 ) , respectively . five of 57 node - positive patients ( 9% ) developed pelvic node recurrences . all five patients with nodal failure had concomitant cervical failure and/or distant metastases . no significant difference was observed with respect to the incidence or severity of late complications by application of boost ebrt . the current retrospective study demonstrated that boost ebrt to positive pelvic nodes achieves favorable nodal control without increasing late complications .

Purpose: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is characterized by chronic intestinal inflammation with unknown etiology. Recent research suggests that gut microbiome plays a critical role in the pathogenesis of IBD. However, the specific mechanisms of IBD are still unclear. This study aimed to investigate the relationship between gut microbiome composition and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

Background: In the current age of an increasing population of elderly people, dementia has become a major public health problem. Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is the leading cause of dementia and accounts for 50–80% of cases (Alzheimer’s Association Report, 2015). The early stage of AD is characterized by the buildup of amyloid plaques, tau neurofibrillary tangles, neuroinflammation, and synaptic loss and dysfunction (McKhann et al., 2011; Villemagne et al., 2013). However, the pathophysiological mechanisms underlying this process have not been fully elucidated, and there are currently no treatments that alter the progression of the disease (Huang and Mucke, 2012). Although significant progress has been made in understanding the etiology of AD, the major challenge to develop disease-modifying treatments remains the lack of a reliable method to diagnose AD in its early stages. Therefore, it is urgent to develop new biomarkers to facilitate early diagnosis and treatment.

Title: Semaglutide as a Novel Weight Loss Agent in Adults with Overweight or Obesity: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Clinical Trials. Background: Obesity is a chronic disease that often requires pharmacological treatment. GLP-1 receptor agonists are effective for weight loss and reducing cardiovascular risk. This review assesses the efficacy and safety of semaglutide for weight loss in adults with overweight or obesity.

the use of guided tissue regeneration ( gtr ) has become a standard of care in periodontology . gtr using membrane barriers and/or bone grafting materials has also been used in periapical surgery . however , the application of the gtr principle , particularly in communicating endodontic - periodontal lesions with loss of the buccal cortical plate , is a very challenging task , with very few reported human clinical studies . an interdisciplinary approach , combining endodontic and periodontal ( surgical ) procedures can save a tooth in the long run that has been defined as hopeless at the preliminary stage of treatment .

Title: Targeting KRAS G12C in Colorectal Cancer: A Review of Recent Progress. KRAS is a highly mutated gene in colorectal cancer (CRC). However, therapeutic targeting of KRAS has proven challenging due to its structural complexity. Recently, KRAS G12C mutant-specific inhibitors have shown promising results. This review discusses the molecular basis of KRAS G12C mutations and the preclinical and clinical studies that have led to the development of KRAS G12C inhibitors. We also summarize the current knowledge on the use of KRAS G12C inhibitors in CRC and their potential future applications in combination therapies.

eyeglasses , required for functional vision by nearly half the world 's population , are still needed by more than a billion people . there are a number of constraints on the provision of eyeglasses : product cost , durability , and appearance ; traditional approaches to evaluating refraction ; and sustainably scaling potential distribution methods . we offer our experience with an immigrant population in a us urban setting using a vision station . the station allowed for immediate provision of adjustable glasses using self - refraction , ordering of custom lenses from a low - cost website , and referral to primary and eye care physicians for those with medical eye concerns . as with models in development by other groups , vision stations connect people with the life - changing provision of functional vision .

INTRODUCTION: The year 2020 saw the COVID-19 pandemic afflict hundreds of millions of individuals globally, and causing the deaths of over 1.5 million people in the United States alone. Multiple COVID-19 vaccines were developed by pharmaceutical companies. However, due to the short time between the onset of the pandemic and the need for vaccines, the efficacy and safety of these COVID-19 vaccines remain a concern.

Background: Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) is a chronic, systemic autoimmune disease characterized by joint inflammation and destruction, and can be highly disabling for patients. Pro-inflammatory cytokines are known to play a key role in the pathogenesis of RA, particularly the cytokine interleukin-6 (IL-6).

Telomere length has been extensively studied in the context of human aging and age-related diseases. While the relationship between telomere length and disease risk remains unclear, there is mounting evidence to suggest that shorter telomeres are associated with increased morbidity and mortality. Previous research has focused primarily on telomere length itself, however, little is known about the role of telomere shortening in driving cellular senescence. The goal of this study was to investigate the link between telomere shortening and cellular senescence, and how this relationship affects cell function.

purposethe proposed men 's health index ( mhi ) aims to provide a practical and systematic framework for comprehensively assessing and stratifying older men with the intention of optimising their health and functional status.materials and methodsa literature search was conducted using pubmed from 1980 to 2012 . we specifically looked for instruments which : assess men 's health , frailty and fitness ; predict life expectancy , mortality and morbidities . the instruments were assessed by the researchers who then agreed on the tools to be included in the mhi . when there was disagreements , the researchers discussed and reached a consensus guided by the principle that the mhi could be used in the primary care setting targetting men aged 55 - 65 years.resultsthe instruments chosen include the charlson 's combined comorbidity - age index ; the international index of erectile function-5 ; the international prostate symptom score ; the androgen deficiency in aging male ; the survey of health , ageing and retirement in europe frailty instrument ; the sitting - rising test ; the senior fitness test ; the fitness assessment score ; and the depression anxiety stress scale-21 . a pilot test on eight men was carried out and showed that the men 's health index is viable.conclusionsthe concept of assessing , stratifying , and optimizing men 's health should be incorporated into routine health care , and this can be implemented by using the mhi . this index is particularly useful to primary care physicians who are in a strategic position to engage men at the peri - retirement age in a conversation about their life goals based on their current and predicted health status .

BNT162b2 is a novel mRNA vaccine developed to prevent severe outcomes due to SARS-CoV-2 infection. This study aimed to evaluate the safety and immunogenicity of this vaccine in a diverse cohort of participants. In total, 360 participants received two 30μg injections of BNT162b2 21 days apart. No adverse events were associated with vaccination. Antibody titers were significantly elevated above the limit of detection by day 14 post-vaccination with sustained seroconversion rates of 97.5% at 28 days post-vaccination. Antibody titers peaked at day 21 and were stable through day 60. Notably, these seroconversion rates were maintained across age, sex, and race/ethnicity subgroups. No significant differences in seroconversion rates were observed among these subgroups.

hydatid disease is one of the relatively common infections in the middle eastern countries . it is seen in areas where dogs are used to raise livestock . in humans , the majority of echinococcus cysts tends to develop in the liver ( 70% ) and is asymptomatic . the two most common complications of hydatid cysts are abscess formation and rupture . furthermore , in 5 - 25% of patients , rupture occurs into the biliary tract and patients may present with cholangitis , jaundice , abscess , or bilio - cutaneous fistula after surgery . intraductal ultrasound ( idus ) is reportedly superior to conventional endoscopic ultrasound for the depiction of bile duct obstruction owing to its additional capability of providing higher resolution images due to the use of higher frequency transducers . unfortunately idus is rarely used , possibly due to the limited availability of appropriate idus equipment , cost of the procedure and interventional endoscopists trained in its interpretation . idus with wire - guided , thin - caliber , high - frequency probes is a promising imaging modality , yet no previous reports discuss its usefulness in hydatid disease investigation . we hereby present the first report of biliary hydatid disease being diagnosed by idus .

KRAS-mutant CRC is associated with higher rates of liver metastasis and poorer prognosis compared with KRAS wild type CRC. KRAS remains undruggable; however, a KRAS G12C (p.C12SC) inhibitor sotorasib has shown substantial antitumor activity in patients with solid tumors with KRAS G12C mutations.

Background: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic inflammatory condition that affects the gastrointestinal tract. Recent research has shown that the gut microbiome plays a critical role in the development of IBD.

Background: Telomere length is a major biomarker of cellular senescence and age. A number of factors have been shown to influence telomere length and cellular senescence, including genetic and environmental factors. However, the precise mechanisms underlying the regulation of telomere length and cellular senescence remain unclear.

The use of biomarkers for early-stage AD could help in the identification of new therapeutic strategies to modify disease progression. Methods: We used data from two independent cohorts to evaluate plasma protein biomarkers for early-stage AD. Data from the Swedish BioFINDER cohort (285 subjects) and the American ADNI cohort (246 subjects) were combined and normalized to improve power and reproducibility. Overview: Type 2 diabetes (T2D) is a serious chronic disease characterized by hyperglycemia and insulin resistance that affects over 400 million individuals worldwide. There are currently many therapies for T2D, including metformin. While metformin has been shown to decrease blood glucose levels and improve insulin sensitivity, it has a short half-life in the body, resulting in inconsistent effects on blood glucose levels. Additionally, metformin can cause gastrointestinal side effects and exacerbate vitamin B12 deficiency.

glomerulonephritis occurs as a rare form of renal manifestation in plasmodium falciparum malaria . herein , we report a case of falciparum malaria - associated iga nephropathy for the first time . a 49-yr old male who had been to east africa was diagnosed with plasmodium falciparum malaria . microhematuria and proteinuria along with acute kidney injury developed during the course of the disease . kidney biopsy showed mesangial proliferation and iga deposits with tubulointerstitial inflammation . laboratory tests after recovery from malaria showed disappearance of urinary abnormalities and normalization of kidney function . our findings suggest that malaria infection might be associated with iga nephropathy .

Background: In recent years, the search for reliable biomarkers for Alzheimer’s disease (AD) has gained considerable attention. Early diagnosis of AD is crucial to improve patients’ quality of life and to determine effective treatment strategies. Several non-invasive methods including magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), positron emission tomography (PET), and cerebrospinal fluid biomarkers have been used in the diagnosis of AD. However, the high cost and need for special equipment limit their use in clinical practice.

Background: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic disorder of the gastrointestinal tract characterized by abdominal pain, diarrhea, and blood in the stool. The exact cause of IBD remains unknown but is likely due to the interaction of genetic and environmental factors. Recent studies have implicated the composition of the gut microbiome in the development of IBD. Our study aimed to characterize the gut microbiome in patients with IBD to identify specific microbial communities that contribute to disease pathogenesis.

Telomere length and cellular senescence are major contributors to organismal aging and development of age-related diseases. In this study, we investigated the relationship between telomere length and cellular senescence in healthy young and elderly individuals. We performed high-throughput sequencing of telomeric DNA to measure the average telomere length of peripheral blood cells. We also isolated primary human dermal fibroblasts from participants of the same age group and induced replicative senescence in vitro. We determined telomere length using a single telomere length analysis (STELA) technique. We further assessed markers of senescence, including β-galactosidase and gene expression profiles. We observed a significant negative correlation between average telomere length in peripheral blood and donor age, consistent with the previous studies. We found that the telomere lengths of cells isolated from elderly donors were significantly shorter than that of cells isolated from younger donors. We observed that replicative senescence was associated with shorter telomere length. Title: EGFR-Targeted Therapy: The Magic Bullet for Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer? Background: Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) is the leading cause of cancer-related mortality, with a 5-year survival rate of only 19% despite advances in diagnostic and treatment modalities. The discovery of oncogenic mutations in Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR) and the subsequent development of small molecule tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKI) have been revolutionary in the management of advanced NSCLC with EGFR mutations. These mutations occur in approximately 15-20% of Caucasian patients with NSCLC, but as high as 40-50% of Asian patients. Methods: TKIs were first tested in clinical trials and found to increase progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) in patients with advanced NSCLC and EGFR mutations compared to chemotherapy alone. The most common adverse effects were diarrhea, nausea/vomiting, skin rash, and fatigue. The first-line use

Background: Aging is the greatest risk factor for most common human diseases. Telomere shortening is a key characteristic of cellular senescence and has been associated with aging and age-related diseases. Methods: We developed an innovative method to measure telomere length and cellular senescence. Our method utilizes flow cytometry and is highly sensitive. Results: We found that telomere length and cellular senescence were significantly correlated with age, age at menarche, body mass index, and smoking status. Furthermore, we identified a novel association between telomere length and educational attainment. Conclusion: This is the first study to comprehensively examine telomere length and cellular senescence in a large cohort of over 35,000 participants. Our findings suggest that telomere length and cellular senescence may be biomarkers of aging.

aimthe aim of this survey was to compare chinese natives and foreign inhabitants in chengdu , china , with respect to : ( 1 ) attitudes towards dental appearance , ( 2 ) subjective orthodontic treatment need , and ( 3 ) the main factors influencing orthodontic treatment need.methodologya total of 522 subjects , including 227 foreign inhabitants and 295 chinese natives in chengdu participated in the survey . a simple random sampling method was adopted and a face - to - face interview was conducted at some public sites using a questionnaire . data was entered by two persons synchronously using epidata 3.0 , and spss 13.0 was used to analyze these data.results89.0% of foreign inhabitants were satisfied with their teeth compared to only 46.8% of chinese natives . females were more dissatisfied with their teeth than males . chinese natives put improving appearance as the top priority ( 55.9% ) for seeking orthodontic treatment ; however , in foreign inhabitants , the main reason for seeking treatment was to improve masticatory function ( 44.1% ) , followed by to be pretty ( 35.2% ) . the importance of well - aligned teeth and self - perception of psycho - social impact of malocclusion were the same two main factors influencing subjective orthodontic treatment need ( p<0.05 ) in foreign inhabitants and chinese natives . subjective orthodontic treatment need between the two target groups was significantly different ( p<0.05).conclusion(1 ) it was very common that chinese natives were dissatisfied with their dental appearance , and their subjective orthodontic treatment needs were high . Rationale: The Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR) signaling cascade is an attractive molecular target in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) due to its importance in the development of the disease. EGFR mutations have been found in a significant proportion of NSCLC patients, especially in never-smokers and adenocarcinoma histology. TKIs are considered the first-line treatment for patients with EGFR mutations. The aim of this study was to investigate the response to EGFR inhibitor gefitinib in the treatment of NSCLC.

The ever-increasing global prevalence of obesity is a public health crisis and a major risk factor for chronic diseases including cardiovascular disease, diabetes, certain cancers, and early mortality. There is an unmet need for more effective pharmacotherapies that provide long-term weight loss and improvements in cardiometabolic risk factors. The gut hormones GLP-1 and GIP are potent insulin secretagogues, and have been used as glucose-lowering therapies for diabetes. In addition, recent studies have shown that GLP-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1RAs) promote weight loss through multiple independent mechanisms including reduction of food intake, decreased meal size, and increased energy expenditure.

Background: Glioblastoma is a deadly brain cancer that is highly heterogeneous in its cellular composition. This heterogeneity poses a major challenge in clinical management and therapy. Our lab has previously identified several single-nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) associated with glioblastoma prognosis using whole-genome sequencing. However, the mechanisms by which these SNPs affect glioblastoma progression are unclear.

Background: Frontotemporal dementia (FTD) is a neurodegenerative disorder characterized by cognitive and behavioral impairments. Understanding the molecular basis of FTD is critical for the development of effective treatments. Aggregation of tau protein, a microtubule-associated protein, is a common feature of multiple neurodegenerative diseases, including FTD. Recent studies have implicated tau protein aggregation in the pathogenesis of FTD, but the precise molecular mechanisms remain unclear.

Title: Targeting KRAS G12C Mutations in Colorectal Cancer: A Review of the Latest Scientific Advancements Background: Colorectal cancer (CRC) is a major global health concern, accounting for over 1 million new cases and 551,000 deaths worldwide each year. Approximately 50% of patients with CRC harbor a KRAS mutation, which confers resistance to targeted therapies and poor prognosis. A recent breakthrough in cancer research has been the development of small molecule inhibitors targeting KRAS G12C mutations, which are the most common KRAS mutations in CRC. These inhibitors have shown promising results in preclinical and clinical studies, offering new hope for CRC patients.

human immunodeficiency virus infection associated lymphadenitis of the salivary glands ( hivlsg ) and benign lymphoepithelial cysts are described as early events in hiv patients . the diagnosis is not usually made clinically as it mimics a salivary gland tumor . we present here a patient with two painless nodules in the right parotid and submandibular glands . the cytological diagnosis was florid reactive lymphoid hyperplasia . histopathology revealed features of hivlsg with multiple , small , cystic , benign lymphoepithelial lesions .

Background: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a significant cause of blindness in working age adults worldwide. With the increasing prevalence of diabetes, the cost of DR to the healthcare system will continue to rise. DR results from a chronic inflammatory process that can eventually lead to capillary nonperfusion and neovascularization. Vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) has been shown to play a critical role in the development of DR, and anti-VEGF therapies have been successfully utilized in treating several neovascular conditions, such as age-related macular degeneration and cancer. Anti-VEGF therapies have also been shown to be effective in treating DR in several randomized controlled clinical trials.

Deep brain stimulation (DBS) has been widely used for the treatment of Parkinson disease. A previous study 1 showed that the functional connectivity strength of Parkinson disease patients was reduced compared with healthy controls. The aim of this study is to explore the effect of DBS on functional connectivity in Parkinson disease patients. A total of 50 patients with Parkinson disease and 50 healthy controls were included. Patients were divided into three groups according to disease duration: ≤3 years, 3–6 years, and >6 years. The DBS-OFF state of Parkinson disease patients was used as the baseline, and the DBS-ON state was recorded after 1-month treatment. Resting-state functional magnetic resonance imaging scans were conducted. The fractional amplitude of low-frequency fluctuations (fALFF) and regional homogeneity (ReHo) were used to measure the local activity of brain function. The ReHo in the DBS-OFF state was lower in patients than in healthy controls, and it was further decreased in patients with longer disease duration. Title: Evaluating immunogenicity of BNT162b2 mRNA vaccine. Background: The COVID-19 pandemic has caused millions of infections and deaths globally. While a number of vaccines have been developed to prevent SARS-CoV-2 infection, we chose to evaluate immunogenicity of the BNT162b2 vaccine, due to its high efficacy.

the authors present the case of a 58-year - old gentleman presenting with atypical carcinoid tumor of primary lung origin metastasizing to a previously identified pituitary macroadenoma . the patient presented with symptoms of headache and visual disturbance . imaging revealed enlargement of a known sellar mass as well as three separate enhancing lesions in the brain parenchyma . resection was accomplished via a transnasal transshpenoidal approach without complication . immunoreactivity was demonstrated to synaptophysin , chromogranin , cd56 , epithelial membrane antigen , and thyroid transcription factor-1 . the specimen was also marked by negative staining for pituitary hormones . this case demonstrates a rare occurrence of metastastic spread of tumor to a previously identified pituitary macroadenoma .

the complement system is one of the key players in the defence against infections . its activation during the innate immune response leads to the generation of several proteins that contribute to the lysis and opsonization of microorganisms , regulate inflammatory reactions and bridge innate immunity with the subsequent adaptive immune response . complement is also activated in overwhelming bacterial infections that lead to sepsis , and its protective functions play a role in this frequently lethal disorder . however , despite its role in protection , complement can also contribute to the development of severe complications that significantly worsen the prognosis of septic patients . therefore , an understanding of the mechanisms involved in the activation of complement during sepsis is essential to our efforts to introduce rational therapies targeting complement to the treatment of patients suffering from this condition . this review presents a current view of the mechanisms involved in the activation of complement in sepsis , in the context of the multiple interactions between complement and other biological systems that are involved in the pathogenesis of this disorder .

Background: Frontotemporal dementia (FTD) is an irreversible, progressive neurodegenerative disorder that occurs between 40 and 65 years of age, with an average age of onset of 58 years. The prevalence of FTD is approximately 20 in 100,000 individuals and the incidence is between 3 and 4 new cases per 100,000 individuals per year. Pathologically, FTD is characterized by the presence of intracellular aggregates of tau protein.

this study is aimed at creating a stochastic model , named brazilian climate and health model ( bchm ) , through poisson regression , in order to predict the occurrence of hospital respiratory admissions ( for children under thirteen years of age ) as a function of air pollutants , meteorological variables , and thermal comfort indices ( effective temperatures , et ) . the data used in this study were obtained from the city of so paulo , brazil , between 1997 and 2000 . the respiratory tract diseases were divided into three categories : uri ( upper respiratory tract diseases ) , lri ( lower respiratory tract diseases ) , and ip ( influenza and pneumonia ) . the overall results of uri , lri , and ip show clear correlation with so2 and co , pm10 and o3 , and pm10 , respectively , and the etw4 ( effective temperature ) for all the three disease groups . it is extremely important to warn the government of the most populated city in brazil about the outcome of this study , providing it with valuable information in order to help it better manage its resources on behalf of the whole population of the city of sao paulo , especially those with low incomes .

Sickle cell disease (SCD) is a hereditary blood disorder that causes red blood cells to sickle. This leads to vaso-occlusion and tissue ischemia, which in turn causes pain crises, acute chest syndrome, stroke, and shortened life expectancy. Current therapeutic strategies only alleviate symptoms; there is no cure. We have developed a CRISPR-based therapy to correct the genetic defect that underlies SCD. In a gene editing experiment using induced pluripotent stem cells from a donor with homozygous HbS, we showed that correcting the single-base mutation in the HBB gene can convert sickle to normal red blood cells. Next, we transplanted the genetically corrected hematopoietic stem cells (HSCs) into sickle mouse models. We were able to achieve therapeutic levels of HbA in the blood of these mice. Importantly, we also found that the corrected HSCs could stably engraft in the bone marrow and provide protection against tissue damage and vaso-occlusion events. Finally, we showed that our strategy is highly specific and does not result in off

purposeto evaluate the effect of various metal oxides on impact strength ( is ) , fracture toughness ( ft ) , water sorption ( wsp ) and solubility ( wsl ) of heat - cured acrylic resin.materials and methodsfifty acrylic resin specimens were fabricated for each test and divided into five groups . group 1 was the control group and group 2 , 3 , 4 and 5 ( test groups ) included a mixture of 1% tio2 and 1% zro2 , 2% al2o3 , 2% tio2 , and 2% zro2 by volume , respectively . rectangular unnotched specimens ( 50 mm 6.0 mm 4.0 mm ) were fabricated and droptower impact testing machine was used to determine is . for ft , compact test specimens were fabricated and tests were done with a universal testing machine with a cross - head speed of 5 mm / min . for wsp and wsl , discshaped specimens were fabricated and tests were performed in accordance to iso 1567 . anova and kruskal - wallis tests were used for statistical analyses.resultsis and ft values were significantly higher and wsp and wsl values were significantly lower in test groups than in control group ( p<.05 ) . group 5 had significantly higher is and ft values and significantly lower wsp values than other groups ( p<.05 ) and provided 40% and 30% increase in is and ft , respectively , compared to control group . significantly lower wsl values were detected for group 2 and 5 ( p<.05).conclusionmodification of heat - cured acrylic resin with metal oxides , especially with zro2 , may be useful in preventing denture fractures and undesirable physical changes resulting from oral fluids clinically .

abstractthe marine medaka , oryzias dancena is a suitable sample as a laboratory animal because it has a small size and clearly distinguishes between female and male . data on the growth and maturity of the diploid and triploid sea cucurbit species suitable for laboratory animals are very useful for studying other species . triploidy was induced in the marine medaka by cold shock treatment ( 0c ) of fertilized eggs for 45 min , applied two minutes after fertilization . the diploid and triploid male fish were larger than their female counterparts ( p<0.05 ) , and the concentrations of thyroid stimulating hormone ( tsh ) and thyroxine ( t4 ) were higher in the induced triploids over 1 year ( p<0.05 ) . in both the diploid and tri - ploid groups the concentrations of tsh and t4 were higher in the male fish than in the females ( p<0.05 ) , while the testo - sterone and estradiol-17 concentrations in the induced triploids were lower than in the diploids ( p<0.05 ) . the gonadosomatic index ( gsi ) of the triploid fish was lower than that for the diploids , and the gsi for females in each ploidy group were higher than that for the males . for both groups the gsi was highest at 4 months of age , and decreased thereafter to 12 months . analysis of the gonads of one - year - old triploid fish suggested that the induction of triploidy probably causes sterility in this species ; this effect was more apparent in females than in males .

the administration of monosodium glutamate ( msg ) to mice induces hepatic steatosis and inflammation . in this study , we investigated the metabolic features of msg - treated mice and the histological changes that occur in their livers and adipose tissue . msg mice were prepared by subcutaneously injecting msg into newborn c57bl/6j male mice . the control mice were subcutaneously injected with saline . another group of mice was fed a methionine- and choline - deficient diet ( mcd ) . compared with the control mice , the msg mice had higher serum levels of insulin and cholesterol than the control mice , whereas the opposite was true for the mcd mice . microvesicular steatosis and inflammatory cell infiltration were detected in both the msg and mcd mouse livers . enlarged adipocytes and crown - like structures were observed in the epididymal fat of the msg mice , whereas neither of these features was seen in the mcd mice . flow cytometric analysis revealed increased frequencies of monocytes and m1 macrophages in the livers and epididymal fat tissue of the msg mice , respectively . the msg mice exhibited the characteristic liver histopathology of nonalcoholic steatohepatitis ( nash ) as well as metabolic syndrome - like features , which suggested that msg mice are a better model of human nash than mcd mice .

to develop an effective and sustainable cell therapy for sickle cell disease ( scd ) , we investigated the feasibility of targeted disruption of the bcl11a gene , either within exon 2 or at the gataa motif in the intronic erythroid - specific enhancer , using zinc finger nucleases in human bone marrow ( bm ) cd34 + hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells ( hspcs ) . both targeting strategies upregulated fetal globin expression in erythroid cells to levels predicted to inhibit hemoglobin s polymerization . however , complete inactivation of bcl11a resulting from bi - allelic frameshift mutations in bcl11a exon 2 adversely affected erythroid enucleation . in contrast , bi - allelic disruption of the gataa motif in the erythroid enhancer of bcl11a did not negatively impact enucleation . furthermore , bcl11a exon 2-edited bm - cd34 + cells demonstrated a significantly reduced engraftment potential in immunodeficient mice . such an adverse effect on hspc function was not observed upon bcl11a erythroid - enhancer gataa motif editing , because enhancer - edited cd34 + cells achieved robust long - term engraftment and gave rise to erythroid cells with elevated levels of fetal globin expression when chimeric bm was cultured ex vivo . altogether , our results support further clinical development of the bcl11a erythroid - specific enhancer editing in bm - cd34 + hspcs as an autologous stem cell therapy in scd patients .

The prevalence of type 2 diabetes (T2D) and its associated complications have become an international public health concern. Inhibition of hepatic gluconeogenesis (HGN) is considered an effective strategy in the management of hyperglycemia in T2D. Metformin is a first-line oral drug used in T2D treatment with multiple mechanisms of action. However, the effectiveness of monotherapy is limited and metformin has been used as an adjunct drug in combination therapies. The aim of this study was to examine the potential of metformin as an adjunct therapy in the treatment of T2D by inhibiting HGN. We used a rat model of T2D induced by a high-fat diet and low dose streptozotocin. After treatment with metformin for 12 weeks, the T2D rats showed a significant reduction in fasting blood glucose levels, body weight, and food intake compared to the control group. We found that metformin inhibited HGN enzyme activity by 35% in T2D rats. Additionally, metformin downregulated the expression of HGN genes in the liver

significant benefits can be derived by heart failure patients from exercise training . this paper provides an evidence - based assessment of expected clinical benefits of exercise training for heart failure patients . meta - analyses and randomized , controlled trials of exercise training in heart failure patients were reviewed from a search of pubmed , cochrane controlled trial registry ( cctr ) , cinahl , and embase . exercise training improves functional capacity , quality of life , hospitalization , and systolic and diastolic function in heart failure patients . heart failure patients with preserved systolic function ( hfnef ) participating in exercise training studies are more likely to be women and are 57 years older than their systolic heart failure ( chf ) counterparts . all patients exhibit low functional capacities , although in hfnef patients this may be age related , therefore subtle differences in exercise prescriptions are required . published works report that exercise training is beneficial for heart failure patients with and without systolic dysfunction .

Sickle cell disease (SCD) is a genetic blood disorder that causes red blood cells to adopt an abnormal sickled shape. This abnormal shape increases the rigidity of red blood cells, reduces their ability to transport oxygen throughout the body, and causes repeated painful vaso-occlusive crises. In this study, we developed a novel therapeutic approach for treating SCD using CRISPR-Cas9 technology. By targeting the HBB gene encoding β-globin, we were able to achieve efficient correction of the β-globin gene in patient-derived induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs), as well as in human fetal hemoglobin (HbF) inducible erythroid cells. Our approach involves a novel delivery system using lipid nanoparticles that allows for efficient delivery of the CRISPR-Cas9 components into the target cells. We were able to demonstrate that this approach is highly specific and does not cause off-target effects. Our results indicate that this approach is a promising therapeutic option for treating SCD.

Title: Metformin: A Promising Adjunct Therapy for Type 2 Diabetes.Background: Type 2 diabetes (T2D) is a complex metabolic disorder that affects millions of people worldwide. Despite advances in medical treatment, many patients continue to experience uncontrolled blood glucose levels and associated complications.Methods: We conducted a large, multi-center, randomized controlled trial to evaluate the efficacy of metformin as an adjunct therapy for T2D. Patients with poorly controlled T2D were randomly assigned to receive either metformin or a placebo, in addition to their standard antidiabetic medication. The primary outcome measure was HbA1c, a measure of blood glucose levels over the previous three months.Results: After 12 months of follow-up, we observed a significant reduction in HbA1c levels in the metformin group compared to the placebo group. Metformin was also associated with a reduction in weight and improvements in lipid profiles. There were no significant differences in adverse events between the two groups.Conclusion: Our study provides compelling evidence that metformin is an effective adjunct therapy for patients with poorly controlled

gitelman 's syndrome is an autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis , hypokalemia , hypomagnesaemia , hypocalciuria , hyperreninemia and without hypertension . gitelman 's syndrome is caused by mutations of the slc12a3 gene , which encodes the na / cl co - transporter ( ncct ) in the distal convoluted tubule . majority of cases manifest during adolescence or adulthood and growth retardation is not the common feature . we report a rare presentation of gitelman 's syndrome in a four - year - old boy with growth retardation .

Title: Early detection of renal failure using novel urinary biomarkers Background: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is a worldwide health issue, and its incidence has been steadily increasing in recent decades. Despite significant advancements in medical technology, there is a lack of efficient and accurate diagnostic tools for early identification of renal failure, especially in the early stages.

Deep Brain Stimulation (DBS) is currently the most effective treatment for late-stage Parkinson Disease (PD) patients. However, the surgical procedure for DBS can cause complications such as neurological damage or death. The aim of this study was to develop a noninvasive DBS approach that can modulate neuronal activity in deep brain regions of PD patients. We used a 7 Tesla Magnetic Resonance (MR) scanner to guide a transcranial alternating current stimulation (tACS) technique to modulate neuronal activity in the subthalamic nucleus (STN) of PD patients. We used a closed-loop tACS protocol to selectively target the STN based on the patients' real-time brain activity. Our results show that our noninvasive DBS approach can significantly modulate the neuronal activity in the STN of PD patients, indicating a potential alternative to the traditional DBS surgery.

the controlled preparation of different crystal morphologies with varying preferential orientation with respect to the substrate is of crucial importance in many fields of applications . in this work , the controlled preparation of different phenytoin morphologies and the dependency of the preferential orientation of those crystallites is related with the preparation method ( solvent annealing vs drop casting ) , as well as the physical chemical interaction with the solvents in use . while solvent annealing induces the formation of particular structures that are partially dewetted , the drop casting technique from various solvent results in the formation of needle - like and elongated structures , with each having a distinct morphology . the morphologies are explained via the hansen solubility parameters and correlated with the solvent vapor pressures . x - ray diffraction experiments reveal preferential orientations with respect to the solid substrate and indicate the surface - mediated stabilization of an unknown polymorph of phenytoin with an elongated unit cell in the b - axis .

recent studies have suggested that the kisspeptin ( kp ) and kissorphin ( kso ) peptides have neuroprotective actions against the alzheimer 's amyloid- ( a ) peptide . overexpression of the human kiss-1 gene that codes for kp and kso peptides in sh - sy5y neurons has also been shown to inhibit a neurotoxicity . the in vivo actions of kp include activation of neuroendocrine and neurotransmitter systems . the present study used antagonists of kp , neuropeptide ff ( npff ) , opioids , oxytocin , estrogen , adrenergic , cholinergic , dopaminergic , serotonergic , and -aminobutyric acid ( gaba ) receptors plus inhibitors of catalase , cyclooxygenase , nitric oxide synthase , and the mitogen activated protein kinase cascade to characterize the kiss-1 gene overexpression neuroprotection against a cell model . the results showed that kiss-1 overexpression is neuroprotective against a and the action appears to involve the kp or kso peptide products of kiss-1 processing . the mechanism of neuroprotection does not involve the activation of the kp or npff receptors . opioids play a role in the toxicity of a in the kiss-1 overexpression system and opioid antagonists naloxone or naltrexone inhibited a toxicity . the mechanism of kiss-1 overexpression induced protection against a appears to have an oxytocin plus a cyclooxygenase dependent component , with the oxytocin antagonist atosiban and the cyclooxygenase inhibitor sc-560 both enhancing the toxicity of a.

cancer remains a major public health problem in many countries . it was found to contain a subset of cancer stem cells ( cscs ) that are capable of proliferation and self - renewal , and differentiation into various types of cancer cells . cscs often display characteristics of chemotherapy resistance and radiotherapy resistance . numerous putative biomarkers of cscs are currently identified including cd133 , cd44 , cd24 , aldh ( aldehyde dehydrogenase ) , and abcg2 . interestingly , no single marker is exclusively expressed by cscs . thus , the various combinations of different biomarkers will be possible to identify cscs , and considerable work is being done to recognize new ones . in order to demonstrate the mechanisms of resistance and response to therapy and predict the outcome as well as prognosis , the ways to track and identify cscs will be extremely important . the technologies of molecular imaging will reveal mechanisms of cancer progression and provide visual targets for novel therapeutics . limited studies were investigated on the detection of various types of cscs by molecular imaging . although the tracking of circulating cscs is still hampered by technological challenges , personalized diagnosis and therapies of cancers are expected to be established based on increased understanding of molecular imaging of cancer stem - like cells biomarkers .

south asian renal transplant recipients have a higher incidence of cardiovascular disease compared with caucasian renal transplant recipients . we carried out a study to determine whether paraoxonase 1 , a novel biomarker for cardiovascular risk , was decreased in south asian compared with caucasian renal transplant recipients . subjects were matched two to one on the basis of age and sex for a total of 129 subjects . paraoxonase 1 was measured by mass , arylesterase activity , and two - substrate phenotype assay . comparisons were made by using a matched design . the frequency of pon1 qq , qr and rr phenotype was 56% , 37% , and 7% for caucasian subjects versus 35% , 44% , and 21% for south asian subjects ( 2 = 7.72 , p = 0.02 ) . pon1 mass and arylesterase activity were not significantly different between south asian and caucasian subjects . pon1 mass was significantly associated with pon1 phenotype ( p = 0.0001 ) , hdl cholesterol ( p = 0.009 ) , ldl cholesterol ( p = 0.02 ) , and diabetes status ( p < 0.05 ) . arylesterase activity was only associated with hdl cholesterol ( p = 0.003 ) . thus the frequency of the pon1 rr phenotype was higher and that of the qq phenotype was lower in south asian versus caucasian renal transplant recipients . however , ethnicity was not a significant factor as a determinant of pon1 mass or arylesterase activity , with or without analysis including pon1 phenotype . the two - substrate method for determining pon1 phenotype may be of value for future studies of cardiovascular complications in renal transplant recipients .

Malignant melanoma is a skin cancer originating from pigment cells called melanocytes, and it is highly aggressive and metastatic. Over the past decade, there have been significant advances in the management of melanoma. Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs), such as anti-CTLA-4 and anti-PD-1 antibodies, have revolutionized the treatment of metastatic melanoma. Anti-CTLA-4 antibodies, such as ipilimumab, act by blocking the cytotoxic T lymphocyte antigen (CTLA-4) and enhancing the immune response to melanoma cells. Anti-PD-1 antibodies, such as pembrolizumab and nivolumab, act by blocking the programmed cell death protein (PD-1) and preventing tumor cells from evading the immune system. Recent studies have shown that the combination of anti-CTLA-4 and anti-PD-1 antibodies can achieve higher response rates and longer progression-free survival compared to anti-CTLA-4 or anti-PD-1 antibodies alone in patients with metastatic melanoma. the posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome ( pres ) is a well described entity of white matter pathology . pres is triggered by numerous different factors such as acute elevated arterial hypertension , immunosupressive therapy , chemotherapy , etc . the case of a 67-year old woman is presented . the patient was treated for breast cancer 10 months ago and because of acute disorientation a magnetic resonance imaging ( mri ) was performed . in the mri biparieto - occipital hyperintense lesions were seen . brain metastases were suspected . after chemotherapy and hypertonia and the typical appearance of the lesions in the mri , pres was also suspected . before initializing the surgery for an open biopsy a follow - up mri had been performed ( 2 weeks after initial mri ) . in follow - up mri the lesions disappeared completely proving the diagnosis of pres . pres can be misdiagnosed as a tumour and surgery could be mistakenly performed . it s important to keep the differential diagnosis of pres in mind when radiologic features of the syndrome are present .

human exposure to chemical compounds , often termed xenobiotics , has been linked to a number of enhanced incidences of various neoplasias . a majority of these enter the human body through inhalation . most xenobiotics are metabolized in the body to more hydrophilic metabolites before excretion in the urine and bile . during this process , promutagens and procarcinogens could be activated and could interact with proteins as well as dna to form adducts . dna adducts formed by chemical carcinogens can , therefore , be used as biomarkers of exposure and other host factors . this study that dna adduct analysis can be performed on cells from human nasal mucosa . using the nasal lavage procedure performed on 20 healthy volunteers , 5 x 10(5 ) to 5 x 10(6 ) cells were obtained from which 5 to 40 micrograms dna was isolated . dna adducts were analyzed by the 32-p - postlabeling assay . the dna adduct levels ranged between 1.4 and 6 adducts/10(8 ) nucleotides . in addition to its simplicity , the nasal lavage procedure is an inexpensive , noninvasive procedure that requires no anesthetics or special equipment . moreover , the cells obtained are the first to come in contact with air pollutants . dna adduct analysis from human nose mucosa cells could therefore be used to develop a technique suitable for the assessment of exposure to chemical carcinogens through inhalation.imagesfigure 1.figure 1.figure 1 .

Background: Metastatic melanoma is one of the deadliest forms of skin cancer. It has a high mortality rate and resistance to chemotherapy and radiotherapy. Recent breakthroughs in immunotherapy have improved the survival and quality of life of patients with metastatic melanoma.

gastrointestinal infections are a major cause of morbidity and mortality worldwide , particularly in developing countries . the use of probiotics to prevent and treat a variety of diarrheal diseases has gained favor in recent years . examples where probiotics have positively impacted gastroenteritis will be highlighted . however , the overall efficacy of these treatments and the mechanisms by which probiotics ameliorate gastrointestinal infections are mostly unknown . we will discuss possible mechanisms by which probiotics could have a beneficial impact by enhancing the prevention or treatment of diarrheal diseases .

Background: Statins have long been established as effective drugs for the management of hypercholesterolemia. In addition to cholesterol-lowering effects, statins have been shown to confer cardiovascular benefits in primary prevention. We set out to further characterize the cardiovascular benefits of statins in primary prevention.

“Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) is a chronic inflammatory disease characterized by progressive synovial inflammation, joint destruction, and increased disability. Although the underlying pathological mechanism of RA remains unclear, the pro-inflammatory cytokine interleukin-6 (IL-6) plays a pivotal role in its occurrence and development. IL-6 is a multi-functional cytokine that regulates a variety of biological processes, such as inflammation and immunity. To understand the role of IL-6 in RA progression, we investigated the effect of IL-6 on the expression of proteins involved in inflammation and immune response in RA patients. We recruited 50 patients with active RA and 50 healthy controls. The protein expression levels of IL-6 and several inflammation and immune-related proteins were analyzed by Western blotting. Our results showed that the protein levels of IL-6 and TNF-α were significantly increased in RA patients compared to healthy controls. Title: Anti-VEGF Therapy for Diabetic Retinopathy: A Comprehensive Review and Meta-Analysis of the Literature. Background: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a leading cause of blindness worldwide, with approximately 93 million people currently affected. As the number of people with diabetes continues to increase, so too will the prevalence of DR. Current treatments for DR, including laser photocoagulation and anti-VEGF agents, are effective at reducing vision loss in some patients. However, they are not curative and do not work for everyone. Methods: A comprehensive review and meta-analysis of the literature was conducted to determine the efficacy of anti-VEGF therapy for DR. Studies published between 2010 and 2022 were included in the meta-analysis. A total of 16 randomized controlled trials (RCTs) met the inclusion criteria and were analyzed using the DerSimonian-Laird random-effects model.
